---
title: "Supplementary Materials for Selection Bias in TEDS Paper "
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var links = document.querySelectorAll('a');
  links.forEach(function(link) {
    link.setAttribute('target', '_blank');
  });
});
</script>

<style>
.section-box {
  background-color: #f8f9fa;
  border-left: 4px solid #3498db;
  padding: 15px 20px;
  margin: 20px 0;
  border-radius: 0 5px 5px 0;
}
.note-box {
  background-color: #fff3cd;
  border-left: 4px solid #ffc107;
  padding: 10px 15px;
  margin: 15px 0;
  border-radius: 0 5px 5px 0;
  font-size: 0.95em;
}
h1 { border-bottom: 2px solid #3498db; padding-bottom: 10px; }
h2, h3 { color: #2c3e50; }
ul { line-height: 1.8; }
a { color: #3498db; }
a:hover { color: #2980b9; }
.title { font-size: 1.8em; }
</style>

---

Supplementary tables with additional information not included in the main manuscript (e.g., exact p-values, confidence intervals) can be found in the links below.

The codebase is structured so that computationally intensive analyses are run using R scripts, with results saved as .Rdata files. Extraction of results and creation of tables and plots is then done in Quarto (.qmd) scripts. The tables and figures from those scripts are linked below.

<div class="note-box">
**Note:** The research question numbering in the codebase differs from how they are presented in the paper.
</div>

---

# Descriptive statistics

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Study Variables](11_descriptives.html#table-describing-each-variable) | Complete list of all study variables organised by research question |
| [Participant Ages](11_descriptives.html#table) | Distribution of age at each timepoint |
| [Maternal Education](11_descriptives.html#education) | Distribution of maternal education levels |

---

# Research Question 1: Predictors of Participation

**Objective:** Identify factors that predict twin participation in the study.

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Variables in Imputation Model](1_rq1_predictors_of_participation.html#variables-in-imputation-model) | List of variables used for imputation |
| [Logistic Regression Coefficients](1_rq1_predictors_of_participation.html#twin-level-logistic-regression-coefficients) | Model coefficients predicting twin participation. N.B. differences in follow-up participation between ethnic groups can be found here. |
| [Education Marginal Effects Plot*](1_rq1_predictors_of_participation.html#plot-marginal-effects-of-maternal-education) | Marginal effect of maternal education on participation |
| [Ethnicity Marginal Effects Plot*](1_rq1_predictors_of_participation.html#plot-marginal-effects-of-ethnicity) | Marginal effect of ethnicity on participation |

\* These [marginal effects](https://marginaleffects.com/chapters/predictions.html) translate the logistic regression model coefficients into  model-predicted probabilities of participation at each time point, which can be simpler to understand. 

---

# Research Question 2: Comparison of Participants vs Non-Participants

**Objective:** Compare outcomes between participating and non-participating twins.

## Toddlerhood Outcomes

### Sample Sizes

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Attrition Sample Sizes (Figure)](plots/pngs/2_attrition_numbers.png) | Sample size per variable at each attrition timepoint |

### Means, SMDs & Variances

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Results Table](2_rq2_results.html#create-gt-results-table) | Means, standardized mean differences, and variances |

### Correlations

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Correlation Matrix (Figure)](plots/pngs/2_rq2_2_correlations.png) | Visual display of correlations |
| [Correlations Table](2_rq2_results.html#correlations-results-table) | Full correlation results |

### ACE Estimates

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [ACE Results Table](2_rq2_results.html#ace-estimates-differences-table-complete) | Heritability estimates comparison |
| [ACE Comparison Figure](plots/pngs/2_rq2_ace_comparison_by_timepoint.png) | ACE estimates by timepoint |

### Maternal Education Supplementary Analyses

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Maternal Education Distribution](2_rq2_results.html#distribution-of-maternal-education-full-sample-vs.-y26-webtest-participants) | Comparison of maternal education distribution between the full sample and Y26 Webtest participants |

## Adolescent Outcomes

### Sample Sizes

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Attrition Sample Sizes (Figure)](plots/pngs/6_rq6_attrition_numbers.png) | Sample size per variable at each attrition timepoint |

### Means, SMDs & Variances

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Results Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#means-smds-variances) | Means, standardized mean differences, and variances |

### Correlations

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Correlation Matrix (Figure)](plots/pngs/6_rq6_correlations.png) | Visual display of attritioned vs. original correlations |
| [Original Correlation Matrix (Figure)](plots/pngs/6_rq6_original_correlations.png) | Unattritioned correlation matrix |
| [Correlations Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#gt-results-table-1) | Full correlation results |

### ACE Estimates

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [ACE Results Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#table---changes-in-ace-estimates) | Heritability estimates comparison |
| [ACE Comparison Figure](plots/pngs/6_rq6_ace_comparison.png) | ACE estimates comparison |


---

# Research Question 3: Evaluating Correction Methods

**Objective:** Assess whether weighting or imputation can correct for selection bias.

<div class="note-box">
A complete list of variables used for multiple imputation can be found in the [following table](8_rq6_3_imputation_results.html#table-of-all-variables-used-in-multiple-imputation)
</div>

## Means & Variances

| Comparison | Link |
|:-----------|:-----|
| Attritioned vs. Original | [Results Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#means-smds-variances) |
| Attritioned + Weighting vs. Original | [Results Table](7_rq6_2_weighting_results.html#gt-results-table) |
| Attritioned + Imputation vs. Original | [Results Table](8_rq6_3_imputation_results.html#gt-results-table) |

## Correlations

### Figures

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Original Correlation Matrix](plots/pngs/6_rq6_original_correlations.png) | Unattritioned correlation matrix |
| [Combined Results Plot](plots/pngs/8_rq6_correlations_combined.png) | Comparison across all conditions |

### Tables

| Comparison | Link |
|:-----------|:-----|
| Attritioned vs. Original | [Results Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#gt-results-table-1) |
| Attritioned + Weighting vs. Original | [Results Table](7_rq6_2_weighting_results.html#gt-results-table-1) |
| Attritioned + Imputation vs. Original | [Results Table](8_rq6_3_imputation_results.html#gt-results-table-1) |

## ACE Estimates

| Comparison | Link |
|:-----------|:-----|
| Attritioned vs. Original | [ACE Difference Table](6_rq6_2_results.html#table---changes-in-ace-estimates) |
| Attritioned + Weighting vs. Original | [ACE Difference Table](7_rq6_2_weighting_results.html#table---changes-in-ace-estimates) |
| Attritioned + Imputation vs. Original | [ACE Difference Table](8_rq6_3_imputation_results.html#table---changes-in-ace-estimates) |

## Fraction of Missing Information

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [FMI for Sample Means](8_rq6_3_imputation_results.html#calculation-for-sample-means) | FMI for imputed sample means at each attrition timepoint |

# Discussion

**Supplementary analyses referenced in the Discussion section.**

| Resource | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| [Maternal Education & Child Outcomes](11_descriptives.html#relationship-between-maternal-education-and-child-outcomes) | Relationship between maternal education level and child academic/cognitive outcomes |
